sources.citation_ieee: [S1] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Vibration," White Paper, 2018, pp. 63-65.

ingestion_notes: This chapter is an outline reference only. A project SOP aligned to UN38.3, IEC 62660-2, or an equivalent applicable standard is required before planning or execution.
Do not present the chapter alone as an executable procedure.

sources.title: Penetration
sources.handbook_title: Test methods for improved battery cell understanding
sources.citation_ieee: [S5] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Penetration," White Paper, 2018, pp. 70-73.

ingestion_notes: Chapter references SAE J2464:2009 as the governing standard.
Chapter provides the clearest procedure out of all safety sections: nail diameter, penetration speed, observation period (1 h with nail in place).
Still requires a project SOP and EUCAR hazard review before release.

sources.title: External short circuit
sources.handbook_title: Test methods for improved battery cell understanding
sources.citation_ieee: [T3] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: External short circuit," White Paper, 2018, pp. 82-85.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [T3] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: External short circuit," White Paper, 2018, pp. 82-85.
ingestion_notes: Chapter provides procedure with short-circuit resistance ≤ 5 mΩ, 10 min short plus 1 h observation.
Multiple standard variants cited: UN38.3 (55 °C, 1 h), IEC 62660-2 (10 min + 1 h), ISO 12405-3 (pack, ≤ 100 mΩ).
Project SOP must specify which standard applies and the exact short-circuit resistance.
